plant_features: Rich source of carbohydrates and protein
Rich source of carbohydrates and protein
symptoms: Small, circular, orange-red spots (pustules) on leaves
Pustules mainly appear on the upper leaf surface
Yellowing and premature death of leaves
treatment: Apply a foliar fungicide at the first sign of pustules. Use rust-resistant wheat varieties for future planting.

plant_features: Primary staple food for over half the world's population
Primary staple food for over half the world's population
symptoms: Oval brown spots with grey or white centers
Spots resemble sesame seeds
Can infect leaves, leaf sheaths, and glumes
treatment: Improve soil nutrient management (especially Potassium). Apply seed treatments and appropriate fungicides before the booting stage.

plant_features: Used for food, feed, and industrial products
Requires warm weather and lots of sunlight
Used for food, feed, and industrial products
Requires warm weather and lots of sunlight
symptoms: Elongated, brick-red to brown pustules on leaves
Pustules appear on both upper and lower leaf surfaces
In severe cases, leaves turn yellow and dry out
treatment: Spray with protective fungicides if rust is detected early in the season. Ensure good crop rotation.

symptoms: Pale green to light yellow spots on upper leaves
Greyish tufts of fungal growth on lower leaf surfaces
Premature defoliation
treatment: Use disease-free seeds and apply targeted broad-spectrum fungicides. Improve field drainage.

symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of the uppermost leaves
Red lesions with white centers appearing on the midrib
Alcoholic smell originating from the cane interior
treatment: Uproot and burn diseased clumps immediately. Avoid water accumulation in the field.

plant_features: Grows bolls containing fibers and seeds
Grows bolls containing fibers and seeds
symptoms: Small brown or black spots on the bolls
As infection progresses, the entire boll rots
Fungal spores or slimy bacterial oozes visible
treatment: Increase row spacing to allow airflow and sunlight to penetrate the canopy. Apply necessary defoliants.

plant_features: Legume crop grown for its edible seeds
Legume crop grown for its edible seeds
symptoms: Brownish-red circular spots on leaves
Bright yellow halo forming around the spots
Severe defoliation leading to yield loss
treatment: Start preventative fungicide spray programs 30-40 days after planting. Practice 3-year crop rotation.

plant_features: Used for human food, animal feed, and ethanol
Used for human food, animal feed, and ethanol
symptoms: Small circular to elliptical red/purple spots
Midrib infection causing elongated red lesions
Stem rot and lodging (stalks falling over)
treatment: Use disease-free seeds and resistant hybrids. Eradicate wild grasses acting as alternate hosts.

plant_features: Major cereal grain for humans, fodder, and brewing
Major cereal grain for humans, fodder, and brewing
symptoms: Dark brown stringy lesions resembling a 'net' on leaves
Lesions quickly join out dying out entire leaves
Drastic reduction in grain plumpness
treatment: Foliar fungicide application is critical at the flag leaf emergence stage. Bury infected stubble deeply.

plant_features: Pungent flavor due to sulfur compounds
Requires cool weather to start, warm weather to bulb
Pungent flavor due to sulfur compounds
Requires cool weather to start, warm weather to bulb
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves turning purple
Concentric dark rings within the purple spots
Leaves breaking over and dying out
treatment: Apply protectant fungicides such as Mancozeb before bulb formation. Avoid excess nitrogen fertilization.

plant_features: Leafy green or purple biennial plant
Grown as an annual vegetable crop
Leafy green or purple biennial plant
Grown as an annual vegetable crop
symptoms: V-shaped yellow lesions on the edges of leaves
Veins turning completely black
Foul smelling rotting of the stem internal structure
treatment: Strictly use hot-water treated seeds. Rotate crops with non-cruciferous plants for at least 3 years.

plant_features: Rich in Vitamin C and Lycopene
Rich in Vitamin C and Lycopene
symptoms: Brown/black spots with concentric target rings
Yellowing around spots
Bottom leaves drop off first
treatment: Remove affected leaves immediately and treat with copper-based organic fungicides.

symptoms: Water-soaked dark spots on leaves
White fungal growth on leaf undersides
Rapid rotting of tubers
treatment: Destroy infected crops entirely to prevent spread. Do not compost.

plant_features: Requires cold winter chilling to fruit
Requires cold winter chilling to fruit
symptoms: White, powdery patches on leaves and young stems
Curling and blistering of leaves
Fruit cracking and rotting
treatment: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate fungicides early in the season. Prune to improve air circulation.

symptoms: Tiger-stripe patterned browning on leaves
Dark spots (measles) appearing on the fruit
Sudden wilting of the entire vine
treatment: No chemical cure exists for internal fungus. Prune out dead wood and protect pruning wounds with paste.

symptoms: Yellowing lower leaves
Black or mushy stems near the soil
Stunted growth and drooping
treatment: Stop watering immediately. Repot in chunky, well-draining soil and trim away black, mushy roots.

symptoms: Reddish-brown lesions on leaves
Soft, mushy patches
Leaves folding or falling over
treatment: Cut off affected leaves below the rot using sterile shears. Keep leaves completely dry when watering.
